The '''Thyrsus Sun Guard''' was a [[mercenary]] unit, formed thousands of [[Standard year|years]] before the [[Clone Wars]], fought from [[22 BBY]] to [[19 BBY]] between the [[Galactic Republic]] and [[Confederacy of Independent Systems]]. In their [[Millennium|millennia]]-spanning history, the mercenaries had often been mistaken for their rivaling [[Mandalorian warrior]]s. After the Clone Wars began, the Thrysus Sun Guard sided with the Republic under the [[Commanding officer|command]] of [[General]] [[Solomahal]]. While fighting for the Republic, they simultaneously worked as [[Dark Lord of the Sith]] [[Darth Sidious]]' secret agents, whose aspects had influenced<ref name="Collapse" /> his<ref name="DBPalpatine">{{Databank|emperor-palpatine-darth-sidious|Emperor Palpatine/Darth Sidious}}</ref> own [[Emperor's Royal Guard|Royal Guard]].<ref name="Collapse" /> Its name was shared with the terrestrial astronomical object [[Thyrsus]]<ref name="Hidden Danger">{{InsiderCite|issue1=203|issue2=204|Starlight: Hidden Danger}}</ref> and [[Thyrsian battle helmet]].<ref name="SoloOG">''[[Solo: A Star Wars Story The Official Guide]]''</ref>

==Behind the scenes==
The Thyrsus Sun Guard was introduced in ''[[Star Wars]]'' [[canon]] in ''[[Collapse of the Republic]]'', a [[2019]] supplement to [[Fantasy Flight Games]]' series of ''[[Star Wars Roleplaying]]'' products.<ref name="Collapse" /> In [[Star Wars Legends|''Star Wars'' Legends]], the [[Sun Guard]] was first mentioned in [[West End Games]]' ''[[Imperial Sourcebook]]'', authored by [[Greg Gorden]] and published for ''[[Star Wars: The Roleplaying Game]]'' in [[1989]].<ref name="Imperial">''[[Imperial Sourcebook]]''</ref> The mercenary group's first appearance was in the action-adventure [[Star Wars: Episode I The Phantom Menace (video game)|video game adaptation]] of [[Star Wars: Episode I The Phantom Menace|''Star Wars'': Episode I ''The Phantom Menace'']], developed by [[LucasArts]] and Big Ape Productions, published by LucasArts,<ref name="Video game">[[Star Wars: Episode I The Phantom Menace (video game)|''Star Wars'': Episode I ''The Phantom Menace'' video game]]</ref> and released for [[Wikipedia:Personal computer|PC]] on [[May 18]], [[1999]].<ref name="IGN">{{WebCite|url=https://www.ign.com/articles/1999/05/18/star-wars-hits-the-shelves|text=Star Wars Hits The Shelves|author=IGN Staff|date=1999-05-18|work=[[Wikipedia:IGN|IGN]]|archivedate=20121121210047}}</ref> The [[Star Wars video games|video game]] included a [[Thug leader]] dressed in black-[[Color/Legends|colored]] [[Emperor's Royal Guard/Legends|Royal Guard]] [[Armor/Legends|armor]]<ref name="Video game" /> who was identified as a Sun Guard in ''[[Evil Never Dies: The Sith Dynasties]]'', an article authored by [[Abel G. Peña]] and published on [[Hyperspace: The Official Star Wars Fan Club|Hyperspace]] on [[June 22]], [[2006]].<ref name="Evil">{{Hyperspace|url=hyperspace/member/insideronline/88/indexp2.html|text=''Evil Never Dies: The Sith Dynasties''|int=Evil Never Dies: The Sith Dynasties}}</ref>
